title: Audio: no pattern labels — recognize, articulate, implement

cues.label: Training wheels off
cues.headline: No labels, no hints
cues.narration: Today the problems arrive without technique labels. You have to figure out what you are looking at before you write a single line of code.

cues.label: Recognition instinct
cues.headline: Ask the right questions
cues.narration: Build a mental checklist: contiguous subarray = sliding window, repeated max/min = heap, all combinations = backtracking.

cues.label: Five-step framework
cues.headline: Read, output, constraints, template, verify
cues.narration: Step one: read fully. Step two: identify the output type. Step three: check constraints. Step four: match to a known template. Step five: verify against examples.

cues.label: Problem 1
cues.headline: Minimum Window Substring
cues.narration: Find the minimum window in s containing every character of t. Substring means contiguous. Coverage condition. This is a sliding window with frequency map.

cues.label: Sliding window detail
cues.headline: Expand then shrink
cues.narration: Maintain need and have frequency maps. Track formed count. When formed equals required, shrink from left. Record minimum valid window.

cues.label: Problem 2
cues.headline: Task Scheduler
cues.narration: Schedule tasks with cooldown, minimize idle time. Most frequent task determines minimum length. Greedy with a max heap or a mathematical formula.

cues.label: Formula approach
cues.headline: (f-1)(n+1) + count_max
cues.narration: The formula: max frequency f, count of tasks at max frequency. Result is max of total tasks and (f-1)(n+1) + count_max.

cues.label: Problem 3
cues.headline: Palindrome Partitioning
cues.narration: All possible palindrome partitions. Return all means enumerate. n up to 16 means exponential is fine. Choose-explore-unchoose backtracking.

cues.label: DP optimization
cues.headline: Precompute palindrome table
cues.narration: Build a dp table where dp[i][j] is true when substring i..j is a palindrome. O(1) lookup inside the backtracking, so the bottleneck is the number of valid partitions.

cues.label: The real lesson
cues.headline: Name the shape first
cues.narration: The first thirty seconds of reading a new problem matter more than the next thirty minutes of coding. Can you name the shape before you touch the keyboard?
